ops.body:
E17 — Golden-set judge calibration: n=30 sizing memo (Noisy-but-Valid, arXiv:2601.20913)

Posted as a COMMENT per the E17 protocol (no edit to the task or the packet). Full artifact + reproduction: ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E17/out/ ; ledger cc-notes log b9830c7.

VERDICT: n=30 (15 warranted / 15 negative) is INSUFFICIENT to calibrate the warrant judge for prevalence estimation at this pipeline's operating prevalence. Two independent reasons.

1) The deployed panel judge has ZERO separation. The pairwise [STEER] vs [STAY_SILENT] judge (cc_steer/retrain/judged.py::panel_labels, opus, seed 1729) voted STAY_SILENT on ALL 30 rows — a constant decider (position-debias coin was 15/15 A/B, so genuine content preference, not slot bias). TPR=0/15, FPR=0/15, Youden J=0. Rogan-Gladen prevalence is UNDEFINED (÷J). Non-identifiable at ANY n until the judge changes. It also fails the packet's own README gate (all-"no" = constant decider, rejected).

2) The pointwise warrant judge (best case) is too weak/uncertain. A direct "was steering warranted? yes/no" opus pass over the same 30 contexts (faithful to the README labeling question) gives TPR=5/15=0.33, FPR=0/15=0, J=0.33 — but at n=15/stratum the 95% CIs are huge (TPR [0.12,0.62]; J [0.06,0.58]). Since J is the correction denominator, the corrected-prevalence CI blows up at the pipeline's likely (high) prevalence.

GOLD CAVEAT: labels.json (human blind labels) DOES NOT EXIST yet — packet authored, awaiting the labeling session. Gold used = manifest.json design-intent strata (the warrant-audit corrected labels). TPR/FPR point estimates are provisional; the sizing conclusion is robust because it is dominated by the n=15-per-stratum binomial floor, not the exact rates.

CORRECTED-PREVALENCE 95% HALF-WIDTH AT PIPELINE SCALE (N->inf floor = the paper's "Oracle Gap": all width is calibration uncertainty):
  pi=0.10 : pointwise delta 0.072 | pointwise VALID 0.094 | pairwise ~0.48 (uninformative; J*<=0 in ~50% of MC draws)
  pi=0.20 : pointwise delta 0.143 | pointwise VALID 0.208 | pairwise ~0.48
  pi=0.40 : pointwise delta 0.286 | pointwise VALID 0.464 ([0,0.93]) | pairwise ~0.48
(VALID = Jeffreys-posterior Monte-Carlo, propagates n=15 binomial uncertainty in TPR/FPR; materially wider than delta — the Oracle Gap. This frame sits at high prevalence: audit pools 79 warranted / 72 negative => pi~0.5, the worst case.)
Pre-registered usefulness bar: 95% half-width <= 0.10 at pipeline scale. n=30 meets it only at pi<=~0.1, and never for the deployed judge.

SIZING — per-stratum balanced n for VALID floor half-width <= 0.10 (Jeffreys-MC):
  Judge quality              pi=0.10  pi=0.20  pi=0.40
  observed pointwise(.33/0)    15       60      180
  target decent(.80/.10)       30       60       60
  strong(.90/.05)              15       45       45
Half-width vs n (observed pointwise, N->inf): pi=0.2: n15=0.208, n30=0.163, n60=0.099, n120=0.060, n240=0.038.
RECOMMENDATION: target >= 60 rows/stratum (120 total) for a useful +/-0.10 prevalence CI at moderate prevalence; ~180/stratum (360 total) if pipeline prevalence is high (pi~0.4-0.5, as the pools suggest). The current 30 rows are a functional gate-agreement screen, not a prevalence-calibration set.

SAMPLING PLAN:
- Fix the judge FIRST — sizing is moot while the deployed pairwise panel is a constant STAY_SILENT decider (J=0). The "most appropriate, least disruptive" framing biases to silence; re-anchor the prompt or switch the gate to the pointwise warrant framing (J=0.33) and re-measure J before sizing.
- Reuse audit.py::run_warrant_audit (200-row stratified, seeds 1729/2718, corrected labels) and enlarge the golden draw via golden.audit_pools/audit_sample to 60/stratum (pools support 60/60 today; 180/stratum needs the audit widened or drawing across other-positive/fired pools with per-stratum labels).
- Keep the blind protocol + hash-pinned packet + rows_sha256-bound labels.json + round-trip _packet_contexts check. Budget ~4 min/row (~8 h at 120 rows); two independent labelers on an overlap subset to estimate the human-label noise floor (which also enters the correction).
- Ship the valid Jeffreys-MC interval (E17 analysis.py) with the audit's Wilson CIs — the gate should consume a calibrated uncertainty, not a single rate.

Approx spend ~$2 (61 opus calls). Repro: vote_pass.py / pointwise_pass.py / analysis.py / sizing_mc.py under ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E17/out/.
